reasoning_trace: The coldStartUntilMs configuration defaults to undefined (accept-on-cold-start mode). For fresh deployments with empty replay cache, a paranoid default (reject-all-until-window-elapses) would provide defense-in-depth. However, the current design is defensible because production redis caches are typically warm. No security vulnerability; recommendation for future hardening.
finding: Cold-start posture defaults to accept-on-cold-start (undefined coldStartUntilMs)
critique: Document best practices for fresh deployments. Consider inverting default for paranoid security posture in sprint-3.
remediation: In sprint-3 polish: add SECURITY.md documentation with cold-start best practices. For high-security deployments, operators can explicitly set coldStartUntilMs at composition time.

reasoning_trace: The in-memory KeyProvider holds a static Map<kid, KeyState> without TTL-based refresh. Production implementations must query JWKS endpoints with 5-minute refresh (industry standard). However, the KeyProviderPort interface is stable and production worlds will implement their own providers. In-memory is explicitly test-fixture only.
finding: In-memory key provider does not implement JWKS cache refresh (no TTL)
critique: Production worlds must implement TTL-based JWKS discovery. The interface is stable; production implementations handle cache strategy.
remediation: For production: implement KeyProviderPort with JWKS discovery (query every 5 min, cache aggressively). In-memory test fixture remains as-is.

reasoning_trace: Rate limiter accumulates buckets for all unique callers indefinitely. For long-running processes (>30 days), memory could accumulate with unique caller spam. However, memory per bucket is minimal (~64 bytes), and the O(unique_callers) growth is acceptable for MCP use case (expected <100K unique callers). Production Redis doesn't have this issue (keys naturally expire).
finding: In-memory rate limiter buckets accumulate indefinitely (no garbage collection)
critique: For development/test fixtures, memory usage is acceptable. Production uses Redis which handles expiry automatically.
remediation: For long-running test environments (>30 days), implement periodic bucket cleanup: e.g., remove buckets not accessed for >24h. Use WeakMap or manual sweep in sprint-3 if needed.
